instructions.instruction: Wave 1 = tasks with no dependencies; Wave N = tasks whose all dependencies are in waves 1..N-1 (topological sort)
original_snippets: Wave 1 = tasks with no dependencies; Wave N = tasks whose dependencies are all in waves 1..N-1
instructions.relevant_when: Mode A Step 4: assigning wave numbers

instructions.instruction: Build an explicit dependency DAG before assigning wave numbers; list every task with its dependencies
instructions.relevant_when: Mode A Step 3: before assigning waves

instructions.instruction: Every task must appear in exactly one wave; no task may be omitted or listed in two waves
original_snippets: Validate — every task appears in exactly one wave; no wave contains tasks that depend on each other

instructions.instruction: A wave is labelled parallel when it contains 2+ tasks with no mutual dependency; sequential when it contains only 1 task or tasks that must run in order
original_snippets: a wave with >1 independent task is parallel; a wave with 1 task (or tasks that must run in order) is sequential

instructions.instruction: Write the wave document to .context/plans/<plan-slug>.md using the format in references/wave-format.md
original_snippets: emit <plan-slug>.md to .context/plans/ using the format in references/wave-format.md

instructions.instruction: Include a Dependency Graph section (ASCII DAG) and a Branch Strategy table in every wave document
original_snippets: Include a Dependency Graph section with an ASCII DAG
instructions.relevant_when: Mode A: always when writing the wave document

instructions.instruction: Include a Verification checklist per wave; NEVER advance to Wave N+1 before Wave N verification passes
original_snippets: NEVER advance to Wave N+1 before all Wave N verification checks pass
instructions.relevant_when: Mode A: when writing each wave section; Mode B: before advancing waves

instructions.instruction: In Mode B, run the verification commands, tick passing checks, update Status column cells, mark the wave heading DONE, then announce which wave is next
original_snippets: Run verification gate — execute the commands listed in the completed wave's Verification: checklist
Mark wave — append — DONE to the wave heading when all verifications pass
Announce next wave — state which wave is now unblocked

instructions.instruction: NEVER put tasks that depend on each other in the same wave
original_snippets: NEVER put dependent tasks in the same wave. If Task B reads output from Task A, they must be in different waves
instructions.relevant_when: Mode A: always during wave assignment

instructions.instruction: NEVER invent dependencies that are not stated in the requirements; parallel is the default when no ordering is stated
original_snippets: NEVER invent dependencies that are not in the requirements. Parallel is the default when no ordering is stated

instructions.instruction: For parallel waves, assign one branch per phase/workstream using the pattern <type>/<scope>-<slug>
original_snippets: For parallel waves, assign one branch per workstream: <type>/<scope>-<slug>
instructions.relevant_when: Mode A: when writing the Branch Strategy section

instructions.instruction: The wave document is the single source of truth for status; NEVER track status inside individual task files
original_snippets: NEVER track status inside individual task files — the wave document is the single source of truth
